memoryArtifacts.headline: tree_Moores_Ancestry
memoryArtifacts.description: This document was compiled by Orville Sullivan L6L2-JJB who is a grandson of Carrie Moore and Delos Sullivan, I was given a paper copy and decided to scan it in and create the searchable text form of the document so others may see and use it. I  tagged a number of the people found in the document to make it easier for others to locate.

memoryArtifacts.headline: tree_Sullivan_Family_Sheet
memoryArtifacts.description:
This family sheet was found in papers of Mildred Evelyn Sullivan LNKH-8PV a granddaugher of Delos.

It was created by her brother Orville Oscar  Sullivan L6L2-JJB who had done a great deal of searching for ancestors. 

Many of the children of Delos appear to have had children.  None were entered at this time (7 July 2020) as I do not know their status

Nte that for Fay William the birth is Sept 21 not 2

zh.lifeSummary: 當Delos Horace Sullivan於1864年5月15日出生在美國伊利諾州桑加蒙斯普林菲爾德時，他的父親William M Sullivan25歲，母親Mary Louisa Vinton25歲。他於1894年7月21日與Carrie Moore在美國愛阿華州法也特結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1920年住在美國明尼蘇達州賀伯特巴度拉 堂史普且於1930年住在美國明尼蘇達州賀伯特托德 堂史普。他去世於1933年11月20日在美國明尼蘇達州卡斯派克 貝 堂史普享年69歲，埋葬在美國明尼蘇達州卡斯卡斯 蕾克潘恩 葛洛夫 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Delos Horace Sullivan于1864年5月15日出生在美国伊利诺伊州桑加蒙斯普林菲爾德时，他的父亲William M Sullivan25岁，母亲Mary Louisa Vinton25岁。他于1894年7月21日与Carrie Moore在美国艾奥瓦州法也特结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1920年住在美国明尼苏达州賀伯特巴度拉 堂史普且于1930年住在美国明尼苏达州賀伯特托德 堂史普。他去世于1933年11月20日在美国明尼苏达州卡斯派克 貝 堂史普享年69岁，埋葬在美国明尼苏达州卡斯卡斯 蕾克潘恩 葛洛夫 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Sullivan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Liam, Donal, James Patrick, Michael Patrick, Aileen, Brigid, Paddy, Siobhan, John Patrick, Kevin Patrick, Parnell.
Irish (Cork and Kerry): shortened form of   O'Sullivan  .

variants.name: O'Sullivan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Donal, Liam, Siobhan, Declan, Kieran, Cathal, Dermot, Eamon, Fergus, Grainne, Seamus.
Irish (Munster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Súileabháin ‘descendant of Súileabhán’, a personal name from súil ‘eye’ + dubh ‘dark’ + the diminutive suffix -án. Compare Sullivan .

nameMeanings.name: Delos
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Santos, Reyes, Angeles, Cristina, Efren, Jose, Orestes, Santos Jose.  French Achille, Jacques, Pierre.

Hispanic: abstracted as a surname from fused preposition de ‘of’ and definite article los ‘the’ (in plural), common components of Spanish surnames (as in e.g. De los Santos ). — Note: It is possible that (in some cases) this is counted as an independent surname only in the population figure published by the US Census Bureau.

Catalan (Delós or Delòs): variant of d'Alós or d'Alòs, habitational names from any of the towns named with those words, as for example Alós d'Isil (in the district of El Pallars) or Alòs de Balaguer (in the district of La Noguera), in Catalonia.

variants.name: De los Santos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Francisco, Carlos, Mario, Armando, Pedro, Raul, Jesus, Roberto.
Spanish: from de los Santos, literally ‘of the saints’, a common element of personal names, originally denoting a child born or baptized on All Saints' Day (see Santos 1).

nameMeanings.name: Horace
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the old Roman family name   Horatius .  The name was once widely used among admirers of the Roman poet Horace (Quintus Horatius Flaccus), but it is at present out of fashion.  See also  Horatio .

historicEvents.title: Assassination of Mckinley
historicEvents.description: President William McKinley was shot at the Temple of Music, in the Pan-American Exposition, while shaking hands with the public. Leon Czolgosz shot him twice in the abdomen because he thought it was his duty to do so. McKinley died after eight days of watch and care. He was the third American president to be assassinated. After his death, Congress passed legislation to officially make the Secret Service and gave them responsibility for protecting the President at all times.

historicEvents.title: Boy Scouts of America
historicEvents.description: When W. D. Boyce was visiting London, he encountered a boy that helped him find his destination. The boy refused the tip that Boyce offered to him and told him that he was just doing his daily good turn. Being inspired, Boyce incorporated the Boy Scouts of America to help teach young men how to have an attitude of service always. Since its foundation, The Boy Scouts of America has become one of the largest Scouting organizations in the United States. Around 110 million people have been participants at some time in their life. The BSA was established to help young people make better choices in life and showing selflessness by serving the community.

historicEvents.title: The Eighteenth Amendment
historicEvents.description: The Eighteenth Amendment established a prohibition on all intoxicating liquors in the United States. As a result of the Amendment, the Prohibition made way for bootlegging and speakeasies becoming popular in many areas. The Eighteenth Amendment was then repealed by the Twenty-first Amendment. Making it the first and only amendment that has been repealed.

historicEvents.title: Oldest radio station west of the Mississippi
historicEvents.description: The Karlowa Radio Corporation, in Davenport, was issued a new license for broadcasting and with it they were randomly assigned call letters of WOC. The small studio was the first to reach the Iowa area and was identified as one of 21 stations that were desirable because of coverage area and performance. In September 1927, WOC became a member of the new NBC radio network and still is today. In 1932, Ronald Reagan got his first broadcasting job at WOC as a sportscaster and he returned in 1988 after his presidency tour. WOC is the oldest surviving broadcasting station in the middle Mississippi Valley and was the first to keep logs on their electrical consumption and their on-air programming.
historicEvents.urlTitle: WOC radio legacy includes Ronald Reagan

en.lifeSummary: When Delos Horace Sullivan was born on 15 May 1864, in Springfield, Sangamon, Illinois, United States, his father, William M Sullivan, was 25 and his mother, Mary Louisa Vinton, was 25. He married Carrie Moore on 21 July 1894, in Fayette, Iowa, United States. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. He lived in Badoura Township, Hubbard, Minnesota, United States in 1920 and Todd Township, Hubbard, Minnesota, United States in 1930. He died on 20 November 1933, in Pike Bay Township, Cass, Minnesota, United States, at the age of 69, and was buried in Pine Grove Cemetery, Cass Lake, Cass, Minnesota, United States.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Delos Horace Sullivan nasceu em 15 de maio de 1864, em Springfield, Sangamon, Ilinóis, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, William M Sullivan, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mary Louisa Vinton, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Carrie Moore em 21 de julho de 1894, em Fayette, Iowa, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele viveu em Badoura Township, Hubbard, Minesota, Estados Unidos da América em 1920 e em Todd Township, Hubbard, Minesota, Estados Unidos da América em 1930. Ele faleceu em 20 de novembro de 1933, em Pike Bay Township, Cass, Minesota, Estados Unidos da América, com 69 anos, e foi sepultado em Pine Grove Cemetery, Cass Lake, Cass, Minesota, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Delos Horace Sullivan родился 15 мая 1864 г. в местности спрингфиелд, сангамон, Иллинойс, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени William M Sullivan было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Louisa Vinton, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Carrie Moore 21 июля 1894 г. в местности феиетт, Айова, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он жил в местности бадоура тоуншип, хуббард, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты в 1920 году и в местности тодд тоуншип, хуббард, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты в 1930 году. Он умер 20 ноября 1933 г. в местности паик беи тоуншип, касс, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности паин гров кеметири, касс леик, касс, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты.
